name: Hoz de Carboneros Trail
name_original: Hoz de Carboneros — ida y vuelta
description: Hike the moderate 5.3-mile Hoz de Carboneros Trail in Montes de Toledo, featuring waterfalls, lush valleys, and challenging terrain.
region_intro: Hiking around Navahermosa offers diverse terrain, characterized by river valleys, historic sites, and varied woodlands. The region features moderate elevation changes, with routes often following natural watercourses and leading to points of interest. The landscape includes areas like the Hoz de Carboneros, suggesting gorges and canyons, alongside more open areas around castles and hills. This provides a mix of shaded paths and expansive views for hikers. Best hiking trails around Navahermosa The most popular hiking route is Hoz de Carboneros Trail, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route navigates through a notable gorge landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castillo de Dos Hermanas and La Milagra Loop, a moderate 8.8 miles (14.2 km) path. This trail offers views of historic structures and surrounding natural areas. Local hikers also love the Río Pusa from Los Navalucillos — circular, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail leading through riverside scenery, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Hiking around Navahermosa is defined by river valleys, historic points, and varied woodlands. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Navahermosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 80 hikers have used komoot to explore Navahermosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Navahermosa?
A: There are 10 hiking routes available around Navahermosa, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Navahermosa?
A: Yes, Navahermosa offers 2 easy hiking trails. One option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019709607/route-of-the-arroyo-de-lanchas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Arroyo de Lanchas&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.8 km long and takes about 1 hour 53 minutes to complete.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on hikes near Navahermosa?
A: The region around Navahermosa is characterized by diverse terrain, including river valleys, varied woodlands, and historic sites. You'll find routes navigating through notable gorge landscapes like the Hoz de Carboneros, alongside more open areas with views of castles and hills.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Navahermosa area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219443515/rio-pusa-from-los-navalucillos-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Pusa from Los Navalucillos — circular&lt;/a&gt; offers a 8.6 km path through riverside scenery. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1196809438/arroyo-de-lanchas-from-hontanar-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo de Lanchas from Hontanar — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 10.3 km trail.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore or landmarks near the hiking trails?
A: While hiking, you can explore several points of interest. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Melque Complex&lt;/a&gt;, a historical site, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menasalbas Village Center&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1196923438/castillo-de-dos-hermanas-and-la-milagra-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Dos Hermanas and La Milagra Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically offers views of historic structures.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Navahermosa?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to woodlands, and the well-maintained paths that lead to historical sites and scenic viewpoints.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty trails for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, Navahermosa has 6 moderate trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1196873647/hoz-de-carboneros-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz de Carboneros Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route, 8.5 km long, navigating through a notable gorge landscape.

Q: How long do the hiking trails around Navahermosa typically take to complete?
A: The duration of hikes varies depending on the route and your pace. Trails range from approximately 1 hour 53 minutes for easier routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1019709607/route-of-the-arroyo-de-lanchas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Arroyo de Lanchas&lt;/a&gt;, to around 3 hours 54 minutes for longer moderate trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1196923438/castillo-de-dos-hermanas-and-la-milagra-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Dos Hermanas and La Milagra Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Navahermosa suitable for family-friendly hikes?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not provided, the presence of 2 easy trails suggests options for families. The shorter, less strenuous routes would be most suitable for those hiking with children.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or offer riverside scenery?
A: Yes, the region features river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219443515/rio-pusa-from-los-navalucillos-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Pusa from Los Navalucillos — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading through beautiful riverside scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Navahermosa?
A: The diverse landscape with both shaded paths and open views suggests that spring and autumn would be ideal for hiking in Navahermosa, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer mornings or late afternoons could also be suitable, especially on routes with more tree cover.
tips: The route through the Hoz de Carboneros begins here, there is a flat area where you can park your car. La Hoz de Carboneros is a beautiful canyon that leads to beautiful waterfalls. It is a practically unsignposted route, except for two signs that we will find on the route.
Beautiful waterfall, one of which hides the Hoz de Carboneros. In the rainy season, you have to be careful, because the rocks can slip.

faqs.question: Where does the Hoz de Carboneros Trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail begins at the entrance of the social hunting ground of Navahermosa. You can easily reach this point by car, and there is parking available at the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The Hoz de Carboneros Trail is generally rated as low to medium difficulty. However, be aware that the final sections become considerably more challenging due to irregular terrain, requiring navigation over rocks and scree, and sometimes even scrambling. The route is also poorly signposted, so a GPS or prior knowledge of the route is highly advisable.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You'll traverse a picturesque valley with Mediterranean vegetation, including oaks, holm oaks, pines, and aromatic scrub. The path often follows the Jimena stream, leading through a gorge with charming waterfalls and abundant water flow, especially after rain. Expect dense fern thickets, wild rose bushes, and spectacular views of 'El Robledo' and San Pablo de los Montes. The final sections involve navigating through rocks and scree, rewarding you with large rock walls and lush vegetation.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in natural beauty. You'll encounter several waterfalls, including a Small waterfall and the impressive Arroyo de la Gimena Waterfall. The route also offers spectacular views from the outset, looking towards 'El Robledo', San Pablo de los Montes, and Los Yébenes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is in spring, when flowers are in bloom and streams are full, or in winter, when low temperatures can create beautiful ice formations. Summer is less recommended due to lack of shade and reduced water flow, which can make the experience less enjoyable.

faqs.question: Is the Hoz de Carboneros Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Given its wild and less-trafficked nature, dogs are generally welcome on the trail. However, due to the challenging terrain, including rocks, scree, and stream crossings, ensure your dog is agile and comfortable with such conditions. Always keep your dog under control, especially in areas with wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this specific trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Hoz de Carboneros Trail itself. It's known for its unspoiled and uncrowded nature, offering a sense of adventure due to its relative obscurity and lack of administrative development.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or fees for hiking in the Montes de Toledo region?
faqs.answer: While the Hoz de Carboneros Trail itself doesn't require permits, it's located in the Montes de Toledo region. If you plan to venture into the nearby Cabañeros National Park, different regulations and potentially fees may apply for certain activities or areas. Always check specific park rules if your plans extend beyond this trail.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging and often wet conditions, especially after rainfall, we recommend sturdy hiking boots that can handle irregular terrain and water. A GPS device or a detailed map is crucial due to poor signposting. Be prepared to get your feet wet during stream crossings. Bring sufficient water and snacks, as there are no amenities on the trail.

faqs.question: Is the Hoz de Carboneros Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Hoz de Carboneros Trail offers both linear and circular options. The linear option is typically around 8 km. The komoot route provided is a round trip, meaning it's a circular route, bringing you back to your starting point.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail provides an intimate connection with nature, allowing observation of diverse flora and fauna. You might spot birds like orioles, hoopoes, and wagtails, as well as various raptors and vultures soaring above. The rich Mediterranean vegetation also supports a variety of insects and smaller mammals.
